refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency that ensures safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is mostly atomic, focusing on teleoperation latency compatible with safety. It is solution-neutral as it does not specify how latency is achieved, only that it must be compatible with safe operation. The phrase 'compatible with safe remote operation' is kept to emphasize the safety requirement without prescribing a specific latency value or technology, maintaining problem domain focus.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the end-to-end communication latency during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify that it meets the safety criteria defined by stakeholders, such as latency p95 ≤ 150 ms.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol optimized for low-latency end-to-end data transfer
Design a system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering
Incorporate adaptive data compression and prioritization to reduce transmission delays
Establish a dedicated communication channel with guaranteed quality of service (QoS) parameters
Use predictive control algorithms to compensate for latency in teleoperation feedback loops
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design a system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering

potential_sources: Tight coupling between communication protocol and processing modules
Shared resources causing delays in data handling
coupling_check.notes: Selected DP focuses on system architecture to minimize latency, which supports independence from specific communication protocols or compression methods, reducing coupling risk.

assumptions_risks: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters to meet latency targets
Operational policies do not introduce additional delays or constraints
Real-time processing capabilities are sufficient to handle data without introducing buffering delays
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including adaptive mechanisms to handle variable network conditions and potential latency spikes to enhance robustness.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core system architecture responsible for real-time data processing and minimal buffering strategies across the system.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Real-time data streams are received and transmitted through the Telemetry interface for processing and feedback.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Network Link interface supports communication with external systems and components, enabling data flow necessary for real-time processing.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as NGHE Solution is designed to handle core data processing; minimal diffusion as most processing logic is centralized here.
Partial containment possible but real-time processing logic would diffuse into other modules, reducing cohesion.
Teleoperation focuses on control commands rather than core data processing, leading to poor containment and high diffusion.
Safety Services primarily handle alerts and safety logic, not real-time data processing, resulting in weak containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary to contain the DP of designing a system architecture prioritizing real-time data processing and minimal buffering, as it centralizes processing logic and minimizes diffusion across other modules.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may cause some diffusion of data processing logic.
Real-time constraints may require tight coupling with Network Link interface, risking performance bottlenecks if not well managed.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If containment risks become significant, consider refining the DP to explicitly separate data acquisition, processing, and communication sub-functions to allow more granular module boundaries within NGHE Solution.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by focusing on a system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering, which directly supports achieving low end-to-end communication latency necessary for safe teleoperation.

diffusion_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may cause some diffusion of data processing logic.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion (containment score 9) and low diffusion (diffusion score 2), indicating that the DP is well contained within this IM boundary. Other modules have lower containment and higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the primary implementation module for this DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible by measuring teleoperation latency (p95 ≤ 150 ms) under agreed network conditions using the Telemetry and Network Link interfaces managed within the NGHE Solution module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the FR to the NGHE Solution IM is clear, as this module encompasses the core system architecture responsible for real-time data processing and minimal buffering, directly supporting the teleoperation latency requirement.

recommended_revisions: Monitor interface complexity and coupling between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Teleoperation modules to prevent diffusion of data processing logic.

If containment risks increase, refine the DP to explicitly separate data acquisition, processing, and communication sub-functions to enable more granular and manageable module boundaries within NGHE Solution.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification and verification feasibility. The DP logically addresses the FR, the DP is well contained within the NGHE Solution IM with high cohesion and low diffusion, and the FR allocation to the IM is clear and verifiable. Although there are some diffusion risks at the interfaces with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, these are manageable through monitoring and do not currently warrant further decomposition. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear and verifiable architecture at this level is preferable to avoid unnecessary complexity.

revise.why: Monitor interface complexity and coupling between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Teleoperation modules to prevent diffusion of data processing logic. If diffusion risks increase, consider refining the DP to separate sub-functions for better modularity.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module's high cohesion and low diffusion scores accurately reflect its architectural quality.
Verification of teleoperation latency under agreed network conditions is feasible and reliable.
open_risks: Potential interface complexity and diffusion of data processing logic with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
Possible increase in diffusion risk if system complexity grows or requirements change.
evidence_needed: Ongoing monitoring data of interface complexity and coupling metrics between NGHE Solution and other modules.
Performance verification results confirming teleoperation latency meets p95 ≤ 150 ms under test conditions.
